terms.definition: Sudden decline in renal function over hours to days, identified by rising creatinine or reduced urine output.
terms.exampleSentence: His sepsis-related acute kidney injury required intravenous fluid resuscitation.
terms.contextNotes: KDIGO criteria stage AKI by creatinine rise and urine output.

terms.definition: Persistent reduction in renal function or structural damage lasting more than three months.
terms.exampleSentence: Her diabetic nephropathy progressed to stage 4 chronic kidney disease.
terms.contextNotes: Staged 1-5 by eGFR; ACE inhibitors slow progression.

terms.definition: Bacterial infection of the urinary tract, commonly caused by Escherichia coli.
terms.exampleSentence: Dysuria and frequency in this woman suggested a lower urinary tract infection.
terms.contextNotes: Nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim is first-line in uncomplicated UTI.

terms.definition: Bacterial infection of the kidney and renal pelvis presenting with loin pain, fever and rigors.
terms.exampleSentence: She was admitted with right loin pain and fever consistent with acute pyelonephritis.
terms.contextNotes: Requires intravenous antibiotics and hospital admission if systemic.

terms.definition: Solid concretions formed in the urinary tract, commonly composed of calcium oxalate.
terms.exampleSentence: Severe loin-to-groin colic and microscopic haematuria suggested renal calculi.
terms.contextNotes: Non-contrast CT KUB is the gold-standard investigation.

terms.definition: Presence of blood in the urine, either visible (macroscopic) or detected on dipstick (microscopic).
terms.exampleSentence: Painless visible haematuria in a smoker mandates urgent urology referral.
terms.contextNotes: Painless visible haematuria is bladder cancer until proven otherwise.

terms.definition: Non-malignant enlargement of the prostate causing lower urinary tract symptoms in older men.
terms.exampleSentence: Tamsulosin was started for nocturia and weak stream from benign prostatic hyperplasia.
terms.contextNotes: Alpha-blockers relieve symptoms; 5-alpha reductase inhibitors shrink the gland.

terms.definition: Renal replacement therapy that removes solutes and fluid from the blood when kidneys fail.
terms.exampleSentence: He commenced haemodialysis three times weekly for end-stage renal disease.
terms.contextNotes: Indications include hyperkalaemia, acidosis and uraemia refractory to medical therapy.

terms.definition: Endogenous waste product cleared by the kidneys, used as a marker of renal function.
terms.exampleSentence: Her serum creatinine doubled over 48 hours, confirming acute kidney injury.
terms.contextNotes: Used in eGFR estimation alongside age, sex and ethnicity.

terms.definition: Selective alpha-1 adrenergic antagonist that relaxes prostatic and bladder neck smooth muscle.
terms.exampleSentence: Tamsulosin 400 micrograms daily improved his urinary flow.
terms.contextNotes: May cause first-dose postural hypotension and intraoperative floppy iris.

terms.definition: Chronic metabolic disorder of hyperglycaemia from defective insulin secretion or action.
terms.exampleSentence: His type 2 diabetes mellitus is currently managed with metformin and lifestyle modification.
terms.contextNotes: Diagnosed by HbA1c ≥48 mmol/mol or fasting glucose ≥7 mmol/L.

terms.definition: Underactivity of the thyroid gland resulting in reduced circulating thyroid hormones.
terms.exampleSentence: Cold intolerance and weight gain led to a diagnosis of primary hypothyroidism.
terms.contextNotes: Replaced with levothyroxine titrated to TSH.

terms.definition: Overactivity of the thyroid producing excess thyroid hormones, classically from Graves' disease.
terms.exampleSentence: Tachycardia and weight loss in this young woman were due to hyperthyroidism from Graves' disease.
terms.contextNotes: Carbimazole and beta-blockers are first-line; radioiodine is definitive.

terms.definition: Acute life-threatening complication of diabetes featuring hyperglycaemia, ketonaemia and metabolic acidosis.
terms.exampleSentence: Kussmaul breathing and ketonuria confirmed diabetic ketoacidosis in this type 1 diabetic.
terms.contextNotes: Treat with fluids, fixed-rate insulin and potassium replacement.

terms.definition: Clinical state of chronic excess glucocorticoid exposure, often iatrogenic from corticosteroid therapy.
terms.exampleSentence: Long-term prednisolone use caused iatrogenic Cushing's syndrome with central obesity and striae.
terms.contextNotes: Cushing's disease specifically refers to a pituitary adenoma cause.

terms.definition: Primary adrenocortical insufficiency leading to deficiency of cortisol and aldosterone.
terms.exampleSentence: Hyperpigmentation and postural hypotension prompted testing for Addison's disease.
terms.contextNotes: Synacthen stimulation test confirms diagnosis; replace cortisol and fludrocortisone.

terms.definition: Oral biguanide that lowers hepatic glucose output and improves peripheral insulin sensitivity.
terms.exampleSentence: Metformin 500 mg twice daily was titrated up for his type 2 diabetes.
terms.contextNotes: Hold in acute kidney injury and during contrast imaging.

terms.definition: Synthetic thyroxine used as replacement therapy in hypothyroidism.
terms.exampleSentence: Her levothyroxine dose was increased after a TSH of 8 mU/L.
terms.contextNotes: Take on empty stomach, 30-60 minutes before food or other medication.

terms.definition: Glycated haemoglobin reflecting average plasma glucose over the preceding 8-12 weeks.
terms.exampleSentence: His HbA1c of 75 mmol/mol confirmed poor diabetic control.
terms.contextNotes: Diagnostic threshold for diabetes is ≥48 mmol/mol (6.5%).

terms.definition: Life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by a dysregulated host response to infection.
terms.exampleSentence: Sepsis from a urinary source was treated with the Sepsis Six bundle within an hour.
terms.contextNotes: Sepsis Six aims to deliver three diagnostic and three therapeutic actions within one hour.

terms.definition: Granulomatous infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, classically affecting the lungs.
terms.exampleSentence: Active pulmonary tuberculosis was treated with rifampicin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide and ethambutol.
terms.contextNotes: RIPE for 2 months then RI for further 4 months in standard regimen.

terms.definition: Retrovirus that infects CD4 T-cells and causes acquired immunodeficiency syndrome if untreated.
terms.exampleSentence: He commenced antiretroviral therapy soon after his human immunodeficiency virus diagnosis.
terms.contextNotes: Universal ART regardless of CD4 count is current guidance.

terms.definition: Acute bacterial infection of the skin and subcutaneous tissue, commonly streptococcal.
terms.exampleSentence: An expanding red, hot, tender area on her shin was diagnosed as cellulitis.
terms.contextNotes: Mark the border with a pen to monitor progression.

terms.definition: Microbial infection of the endocardial heart surface, typically affecting valves.
terms.exampleSentence: Persistent fever, splinter haemorrhages and a new murmur suggested infective endocarditis.
terms.contextNotes: Modified Duke criteria guide diagnosis with blood cultures and echocardiography.

terms.definition: Gram-positive anaerobic bacterium causing antibiotic-associated colitis with toxin-mediated diarrhoea.
terms.exampleSentence: Profuse diarrhoea after broad-spectrum antibiotics raised suspicion of Clostridium difficile colitis.
terms.contextNotes: Oral vancomycin or fidaxomicin is preferred over metronidazole for first episode.

terms.definition: Parasitic infection by Plasmodium species transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes.
terms.exampleSentence: Cyclical fevers in a returning traveller from West Africa raised concern for falciparum malaria.
terms.contextNotes: Severe falciparum malaria is treated with intravenous artesunate.

terms.definition: Acute lower respiratory tract infection causing alveolar inflammation and consolidation.
terms.exampleSentence: Right basal crackles and fever led to admission with community-acquired pneumonia.
terms.contextNotes: CURB-65 score guides admission and antibiotic intensity.

terms.definition: Combination antibiotic of amoxicillin and clavulanic acid effective against beta-lactamase producers.
terms.exampleSentence: Co-amoxiclav 625 mg three times daily was started for her pyelonephritis.
terms.contextNotes: Increased risk of C. difficile and cholestatic hepatitis.

terms.definition: Microbiological investigation to detect bacteraemia by incubating venous blood in culture media.
terms.exampleSentence: Two sets of blood cultures were taken before antibiotics were administered for sepsis.
terms.contextNotes: Take aerobic and anaerobic bottles before starting empirical antibiotics.

terms.definition: Urinary antiseptic active against most uropathogens and used for uncomplicated lower UTI.
terms.exampleSentence: Nitrofurantoin 100 mg twice daily was prescribed for her uncomplicated cystitis.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in eGFR <45 ml/min and at term in pregnancy.
